personal.name: Manita Crawley, SSCP
personal.title: IT Security Engineer
personal.description: Cybersecurity professional specializing in Zero Trust Architecture, cloud security, and identity governance. Translating security frameworks into practical, compliant implementations.

projects.name: Privileged Identity Management Implementation
projects.objective: Implement Just-in-Time access to enforce the principle of least privilege and reduce standing privileges
method: Audited all admin roles in Entra ID and M365
Identified excess permissions based on job role and designed role governance strategy
Configured role settings with activation requirements and approval workflows
Converted permanent assignments to eligible with defined approvers
Tested activation workflows (self-activation and approval-required)
Configured quarterly access reviews
Mapped controls to SOX 404 and PCI DSS Requirement 7

artifacts.name: PIM Role Assignments
artifacts.description: Role identification and configuration through each stage of the project.

artifacts.name: PIM Portfolio Report
artifacts.description: Portfolio write-up of project objectives, methodology, and outcome.

projects.name: Zero Trust Architecture Gap Analysis
projects.objective: Conduct security assessment against NIST SP 800-207 to identify gaps and develop remediation roadmap
method: Inventoried all digital and assumed physical assets
Mapped existing security boundaries and trust relationships
Identified sensitive data flows to pinpoint vulnerabilities
Compared current state vs NIST SP 800-207 ZT principles
Scored maturity using 1-5 scale across 6 control domains
Applied Likelihood × Impact methodology for risk assessment
Mapped gaps to ISO 27001, PCI DSS, and CISA ZT Model
Prioritized by risk and created phased remediation plan

artifacts.name: ZTA Gap Analysis
artifacts.description: Portfolio write-up of project objectives, methodology, and outcome.

projects.name: MFP Service Account Hardening
projects.objective: Harden a shared MFP scan-to-email service account across 20 printers at 10 sites where MFA wasn't supported, using Conditional Access and compensating controls
method: Generated and vaulted an 18-character random password in IT Glue with SSO-gated access
Disabled SSPR to prevent attacker-driven password resets during incident response
Assigned no Entra ID roles to keep the blast radius minimal
Restricted mail flow to intra-organizational senders and recipients only
Stripped the license down to Exchange Online, Defender for Office 365, and Entra ID P1
Removed all mail authentication methods except Authenticated SMTP
Deployed three layered Conditional Access policies: country-based, IP-based, and legacy auth block
skills: Mail flow and Exchange Online configuration

artifacts.name: MFP Hardening Portfolio Writeup
artifacts.description: Full project writeup covering the scenario, environment, account hardening steps, Conditional Access policy design, and defense-in-depth reasoning.

projects.name: SQL Injection Vulnerability Assessment
projects.objective: Identify and document SQL injection vulnerabilities in web application authentication and search functions
method: Tested authentication and search functionality for injection points
Documented exploitation with screenshots and SQL query analysis
Calculated CVSS v3.1 risk scores for each vulnerability
Analyzed SQL queries to identify injection mechanisms
Developed remediation using a layered defense strategy
Mapped findings to OWASP Top 10 A03:Injection
Aligned recommendations to PCI DSS 6.5.1 and NIST SP 800-53

artifacts.name: Risk Register
artifacts.description: Risk register entry for SQLi finding and recommendation.

artifacts.name: SQL Injection Portfolio Report
artifacts.description: Portfolio write-up of project objectives, methodology, and outcome.

artifacts.name: Web Application Scan Report
artifacts.description: Findings for web application scan performed by Wapiti.

projects.name: Email Security Analyzer CLI
projects.objective: Build a threat intelligence tool integrating VirusTotal, AbuseIPDB, DNS validation, and Google's Custom Search for efficient phishing investigation
method: Integrated multiple threat intelligence APIs
Automated email header parsing and analysis
Built reputation checking with scoring
Created formatted CLI output
Reduced investigation time from 15 to 7.5 minutes

artifacts.name: CLI Tool Overview
artifacts.description: Summary of planned and implemented security measures for data and user protection.

artifacts.name: API Integrations
artifacts.description: Threat intelligence sourcing and their utilization in this tool.

projects.name: Enterprise Vulnerability Management Program
projects.objective: Establish a full vulnerability management program using NIST frameworks for an enterprise environment as my capstone project
method: Designed a virtual environment to mimic a small enterprise
Deployed OpenVAS and OWASP ZAP for comprehensive scanning
Created risk matrix (5x5) for likelihood/impact scoring
Built Excel-based risk register tracking 40+ vulnerabilities
Implemented 7-phase cyclical methodology (NIST-aligned)
Achieved 100% remediation of critical/high-risk vulnerabilities
Reduced IT security posture from reactive to proactive

artifacts.name: Methodology Flowchart
artifacts.description: Seven-phase cyclical process diagram in alignment with NIST 800-30.

artifacts.name: Risk Matrix
artifacts.description: Likelihood vs. impact scoring matrix with color-coded risk levels (Very Low, Low, Medium, High, Very High, Extreme) for vulnerability prioritization.

artifacts.name: Risk Register
artifacts.description: Excel-based tracking system documenting vulnerability IDs, affected assets, risk scores, remediation actions, ownership, and completion status.

artifacts.name: Network Diagram
artifacts.description: VirtualBox lab environment architecture showing Windows and Linux systems, IP addressing scheme, domain controller, web server, and network segmentation with screened submit.

artifacts.name: Full Capstone Report
artifacts.description: Complete project documentation including problem summary, IT solution, implementation plan, methodology, timeline analysis, and success evaluation.
